req.title: Joint account operations
req.statement: A joint-account holder shall perform transactions; the secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership.
error_pathways: 403 when secondary holder attempts ownership change

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines two distinct actor roles (primary and secondary joint-account holders) and two operation categories (transactions vs. ownership changes). EP partitions actors into primary/secondary and operations into allowed/forbidden. BVA targets the role boundary where a caller is exactly one role and not another. STC covers the state transition from 'pending transaction' to 'applied'. NEG confirms 403 on forbidden ownership mutation. DT cross-maps actor x operation to expected outcome. Together these four heuristics yield a minimum suite of five deterministic, idempotent tests.

tests.name: test_primary_holder_can_perform_transaction
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as primary holder with token <PRIMARY_TOKEN>
Account has sufficient balance for the transaction
expected: Transaction id is present in response
trace: R-FIN-018: primary holder shall perform transactions
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api"
ACCOUNT_ID = "<ACCOUNT_ID>"
PRIMARY_TOKEN = "<PRIMARY_TOKEN>"


def test_primary_holder_can_perform_transaction():
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {PRIMARY_TOKEN}"}
    payload = {"type": "debit", "amount": 100.00}
    url = f"{BASE_URL}/accounts/{ACCOUNT_ID}/joint"

    response = requests.post(url, json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "applied"
    assert "transaction_id" in body

tests.name: test_secondary_holder_cannot_modify_ownership
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as secondary holder with token <SECONDARY_TOKEN>
expected: Response body error message indicates insufficient privileges
trace: R-FIN-018: secondary holder shall NOT modify ownership
R-FIN-018: error pathway = 403 when secondary holder attempts ownership change
tests.executable:
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api"
ACCOUNT_ID = "<ACCOUNT_ID>"
SECONDARY_TOKEN = "<SECONDARY_TOKEN>"
NEW_USER_ID = "<NEW_USER_ID>"


def test_secondary_holder_cannot_modify_ownership():
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {SECONDARY_TOKEN}"}
    payload = {"action": "add_owner", "user_id": NEW_USER_ID}
    url = f"{BASE_URL}/accounts/{ACCOUNT_ID}/joint"

    response = requests.patch(url, json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert response.status_code == 403
    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body

tests.name: test_secondary_holder_at_role_boundary_can_transact_not_own
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as secondary holder with token <SECONDARY_TOKEN>
Caller has exactly secondary-holder role (no elevated privileges)
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/joint with body {"type": "credit", "amount": 0.01} using SECONDARY_TOKEN to verify transaction is permitted
PATCH /accounts/{id}/joint with body {"action": "remove_owner", "user_id": "<PRIMARY_USER_ID>"} using SECONDARY_TOKEN to verify ownership change is blocked
trace: R-FIN-018: boundary between allowed (transaction) and forbidden (ownership) operations for secondary holder
R-FIN-018: BVA on role permission boundary
tests.executable:
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api"
ACCOUNT_ID = "<ACCOUNT_ID>"
SECONDARY_TOKEN = "<SECONDARY_TOKEN>"
PRIMARY_USER_ID = "<PRIMARY_USER_ID>"


def test_secondary_holder_at_role_boundary_can_transact_not_own():
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {SECONDARY_TOKEN}"}
    url = f"{BASE_URL}/accounts/{ACCOUNT_ID}/joint"

    txn_response = requests.post(url, json={"type": "credit", "amount": 0.01}, headers=headers)
    assert txn_response.status_code == 200
    assert txn_response.json().get("status") == "applied"

    own_payload = {"action": "remove_owner", "user_id": PRIMARY_USER_ID}
    own_response = requests.patch(url, json=own_payload, headers=headers)
    assert own_response.status_code == 403

tests.name: test_transaction_state_transitions_pending_to_applied
preconditions: Joint account exists with id=<ACCOUNT_ID> in state=active
Caller is authenticated as primary holder with token <PRIMARY_TOKEN>
No pending transaction exists on the account
expected: GET returns transaction with state=applied (not pending or failed)
trace: R-FIN-018: trigger = holder performs operation; expected outcome = operation applied
R-FIN-018: STC from transaction-initiated to transaction-applied
tests.executable:
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api"
ACCOUNT_ID = "<ACCOUNT_ID>"
PRIMARY_TOKEN = "<PRIMARY_TOKEN>"


def test_transaction_state_transitions_pending_to_applied():
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {PRIMARY_TOKEN}"}
    post_url = f"{BASE_URL}/accounts/{ACCOUNT_ID}/joint"

    post_resp = requests.post(post_url, json={"type": "debit", "amount": 50.00}, headers=headers)
    assert post_resp.status_code == 200
    body = post_resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "applied"

    txn_id = body.get("transaction_id")
    assert txn_id is not None

    get_url = f"{BASE_URL}/accounts/{ACCOUNT_ID}/joint/transactions/{txn_id}"
    get_resp = requests.get(get_url, headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json().get("state") == "applied"

tests.name: test_decision_table_actor_operation_outcome_matrix
preconditions: Primary token <PRIMARY_TOKEN> and secondary token <SECONDARY_TOKEN> are both valid
Ownership change payload targets add_owner action
actions: Primary holder POSTs a transaction (allowed combination)
Secondary holder POSTs a transaction (allowed combination)
Primary holder PATCHes ownership (primary is allowed per domain rules)
trace: R-FIN-018: DT cross-product of {primary, secondary} x {transaction, ownership_change}
R-FIN-018: only secondary x ownership_change must be blocked
tests.executable:
import requests
import pytest

BASE_URL = "http://banking-api"
ACCOUNT_ID = "<ACCOUNT_ID>"
PRIMARY_TOKEN = "<PRIMARY_TOKEN>"
SECONDARY_TOKEN = "<SECONDARY_TOKEN>"
NEW_USER_ID = "<NEW_USER_ID>"


def _headers(token):
    return {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}


def test_decision_table_actor_operation_outcome_matrix():
    url = f"{BASE_URL}/accounts/{ACCOUNT_ID}/joint"

    r1 = requests.post(url, json={"type": "debit", "amount": 10.00}, headers=_headers(PRIMARY_TOKEN))
    assert r1.status_code == 200, f"primary txn failed: {r1.text}"

    r2 = requests.post(url, json={"type": "debit", "amount": 10.00}, headers=_headers(SECONDARY_TOKEN))
    assert r2.status_code == 200, f"secondary txn failed: {r2.text}"

    own_payload = {"action": "add_owner", "user_id": NEW_USER_ID}
    r3 = requests.patch(url, json=own_payload, headers=_headers(PRIMARY_TOKEN))
    assert r3.status_code == 200, f"primary ownership change failed: {r3.text}"

    r4 = requests.patch(url, json=own_payload, headers=_headers(SECONDARY_TOKEN))
    assert r4.status_code == 403, f"secondary ownership change should be 403, got {r4.status_code}"
